modules.name: rxdjango-env.bin.activate_this
documentation:
Activate virtualenv for current interpreter:

Use exec(open(this_file).read(), {'__file__': this_file}).

This can be used when you must use an existing Python interpreter, not the virtualenv bin/python.

variables.name: rxdjango-env.bin.activate_this.base
documentation: strip away the bin part from the __file__, plus the path separator

and almost to HTML 4.01 transitional (except for closing empty tags).

modules.name: rxdjango-env.bin.rst2odt_prepstyles
documentation:
Adapt a word-processor-generated styles.odt for odtwriter use:

Drop page size specifications from styles.xml in STYLE_FILE.odt.
See <https://docutils.sourceforge.io/docs/user/odt.html#page-size>

Provisional backwards compatibility stub (to be removed in Docutils >= 0.21).

The actual code moved to the "docutils" library package and can be started
with `python -m docutils.writers.odf_odt.prepstyles`.

classes.name: rxdjango.state_loader.StateLoader

methods.name: rxdjango.state_loader.StateLoader._user_filter
documentation:
Filter out other user's instances.
During COLD, HEATING and COOLING loader has to deal with all users

methods.name: rxdjango.state_loader.StateLoader._list_instances_cold
documentation:
In COLD state, fetch all instances from database,
write to mongo and redis and send instances filtered by user

methods.name: rxdjango.state_loader.StateLoader._list_instances_heating
documentation:
In HEATING state, fetch all instances from redis and
send instances filtered by user.

methods.name: rxdjango.state_loader.StateLoader._list_instances_hot
documentation: In HOT state, fetch filtered instances from mongo and send

methods.name: rxdjango.state_loader.StateLoader._list_instances_cooling
documentation:
In COOLING state, fetch all instances from redis, write to mongo
and send instances filtered by user.

methods.name: rxdjango.state_loader.StateLoader.list_instances
documentation: All instances that should go to a user

Manages the cache synchronization process using Redis.
Cache has 4 possible states, and each time a client access it, a transition
may occur. These are:

- 0 COLD: No cache, need to get from db
- 1 HEATING: Cache is being built, peers can follow at redis
- 2 HOT: Cache is available, everyone gets from mongo
- 3 COOLING: Cache is being deleted, first peer can re-heat from redis

Attributes:
    name (str): The name of the cache.
    anchor_id (int): The anchor ID associated with the cache.
    base_key (str): The base key used for all Redis keys related to this anchor ID.

functions.name: rxdjango.ts.export_interface
documentation: Mark this serializer so that its interface will be exported

functions.name: rxdjango.ts.ts_exported
documentation: Check if a serializer interface should be exported as TS

functions.name: rxdjango.ts.channels.list_consumer_patterns
documentation:
Function to extract WebSocket consumers from the root routing (or provided router)
for a specific app that extends the StateConsumer class.

functions.name: rxdjango.ts.interfaces.__process_field
documentation: Generates and returns a tuple representing the Typescript field name and Type.

functions.name: rxdjango.ts.interfaces.__map_choices_to_union
documentation: Generates and returns a TS union type for all values in the provided choices OrderedDict

functions.name: rxdjango.ts.interfaces.serialize_type
documentation:
Generates and returns a Typescript Interface by iterating
through the serializer fields of the DRF Serializer class
passed in as a parameter, and mapping them to the appropriate Typescript
data type.

methods.name: rxdjango.redis.RedisStateSession.load
documentation:
Make an atomic call to redis, check the state and maybe transition

States are:
  COLD
    - Documents are not cached.
    - Transition to HEATING
    - Clear the Redis instance list and set readers to 0
    - State come from ORM and go to Redis and Mongo
  HEATING
    - Documents are being cached
    - Increment readers
    - State come from Redis
  HOT
    - Documents are cached
    - State come from Mongo
  COOLING
    - Documents are being deleted from Mongo and availabe in Redis
    - Transition to HEATING
    - Increment readers
    - State come from Redis and go back go Mongo

access_time is always updated

Returns:
    int: The current state, represented as one of the class constants.

methods.name: rxdjango.redis.RedisStateSession.end_cold_session
documentation:
End a COLD session

Transition state to HOT and delete the instances key if readers is 0.

methods.name: rxdjango.redis.RedisStateSession.end_heating_session
documentation:
End a HEATING session

Decrement readers and delete the instances key if readers reaches 0.

methods.name: rxdjango.redis.RedisStateSession.end_hot_session
documentation: HOT session doesn't change anything

methods.name: rxdjango.redis.RedisStateSession.end_cooling_session
documentation:
End COOLING session.

Since we setup readers at 1, this is the same as end_heating_session.

methods.name: rxdjango.redis.RedisStateSession.rollback_to_cold
documentation: Transition to COLD, and broadcast error message to readers

methods.name: rxdjango.redis.RedisStateSession.write_instances
documentation:
Serialize a list of objects, append them to the instances list, and publish the size to the trigger pub/sub channel.

Args:
    objects (list): List of objects to append to the instances list.

methods.name: rxdjango.redis.RedisStateSession.end_write
documentation: Notify all readers with a negative length, or delete instances if no readers

methods.name: rxdjango.redis.RedisStateSession.list_instances
documentation:
An async generator that yields all instances available, then awaits on trigger and yields all new instances.

When a negative value comes, it raises StopAsyncIteration after yielding the instances.

classes.name: rxdjango.redis._LockContextManager
documentation:
This is a base class for the two context managers that lock
one channel for writing or cleanup.
It manages the keys 6 and 7 and implements a semaphore to avoid
cache cleanup while instances are being written.

classes.name: rxdjango.redis.WriteLock
documentation:
A non-exclusive lock on a channel to avoid cleanup while data is
being written to mongo. Increments relay_writers (KEYS[6]) during
write operation.

classes.name: rxdjango.redis.CleanupLock
documentation:
An exclusive lock on a channel to avoid new writes to mongo during
cleanup. It sets relay_writers to negative number.

methods.name: rxdjango.signal_handler.SignalHandler._relay
documentation: Send one update for both cache and connected clients

methods.name: rxdjango.apps.ReactFrameworkConfig.ready
documentation: Discover and register ContextChannel subclasses within Django apps.

classes.name: rxdjango.channels.ContextChannelMeta

methods.name: rxdjango.channels.ContextChannelMeta.__new__
documentation: Create and return a new class instance.

Metaclass for the ContextChannel.

Builds the state model based on the provided fields and Meta class,
and assigns a WebsocketRouter and a SignalHandler to it.

classes.name: rxdjango.channels.ContextChannel

methods.name: rxdjango.channels.ContextChannel.get_anchor_id
documentation:
Subclass may implement get_anchor_id, based on url parameters,
otherwise the first parameter will be assumed to be it

methods.name: rxdjango.channels.ContextChannel.has_permission
documentation: Implement this method to check if user has permission on a channel

methods.name: rxdjango.channels.ContextChannel.on_connect
documentation:
Called after user has been authenticated.
tstamp is the tstamp sent on connection, if this is a reconnection

methods.name: rxdjango.channels.ContextChannel.on_disconnect
documentation: Called when user disconnects

methods.name: rxdjango.channels.ContextChannel.receive
documentation: Any data sent by user will get here

This is the core API of RxDjango.
ContextChannel provides a state that is synchronized with
all connected clients through a websocket.
Frontend classes are automatically generated for each
ContextChannel subclass.
Subclasses of ContextChannel should reside in a file named
channels.py inside a django app.

classes.name: rxdjango.consumers.StateConsumer

methods.name: rxdjango.consumers.StateConsumer.connect
documentation: Accept any connection and just wait for a token.

WebSocket consumer that manages user authentication, session management,
and real-time data relay to clients. A subclass of StateConsumer will be
dinamically created by ContextChannel.as_asgi().
